title: Publish shared automation routing precedence across browser and scraper playbooks
summary: Align routing precedence in browser-automation-playbook, web-scraper, and x-browser-automation-playbook to remove overlap ambiguity.
details:
Apply consistent precedence rules across automation skills.

Scope:
1. Update `skills/browser-automation-playbook/SKILL.md` with explicit “when this is canonical” boundaries.
2. Update `skills/web-scraper/SKILL.md` with complementary boundaries (static extraction vs interactive/session workflows).
3. Ensure `skills/x-browser-automation-playbook/SKILL.md` mirrors the same cross-skill precedence model.
4. Keep the precedence language mutually consistent across all three files.

Acceptance:
- All three skills include aligned routing precedence text.
- No contradictory guidance remains.

affectedFiles.description: Add explicit canonical-use precedence and boundaries.
Add complementary precedence and boundaries vs browser playbooks.
Align X-specific precedence language with shared model.

diffPreview:
+ Shared precedence contract across browser/scraper/X skills to prevent misrouting.
~ Boundary sections added/normalized in three automation skills.
estimatedImpact: Reduces skill selection ambiguity and improves deterministic automation behavior.
executorPrompt: Update `skills/browser-automation-playbook/SKILL.md`, `skills/web-scraper/SKILL.md`, and `skills/x-browser-automation-playbook/SKILL.md` to include a consistent routing precedence model: interactive/session browser tasks -> browser playbook, static extraction/crawl -> web-scraper, x.com-specific interactive flows -> X playbook. Ensure wording is aligned and non-contradictory across all three files. Return the final precedence block from each file.
